## [0.8.0] - 2026-07-19

A charts-and-settings release. Two threads landed together: the app's charting was unified onto a single Canvas primitive (the **N3** backlog item), and the lone "Appearance" panel grew into a data-driven, four-category settings system with a dozen new preferences. The bulk of the change is new React/TypeScript in the app (`/app`). Existing settings and workspaces carry over unchanged.

### Added
- **Unified, interactive charts.** Every chart — live dashboard and history — now renders through one Canvas (uPlot) primitive via 8 semantic components (latency percentiles, latency breakdown, request rate with a target line and ramp overlay, connections, error rate, response-time-vs-concurrency scatter, stacked status-codes-over-time, and the HDR distribution). One hover places a **synced cursor across every chart**, each showing its value at that instant; drag to zoom the moment of degradation, and the capacity breakpoint is drawn inline. Charts theme from the app's CSS design tokens and re-read on light/dark flip.
- **App settings, reorganized and expanded.** App Settings is now four focused categories — **Appearance**, **Editor**, **Dashboard**, and **General** — driven by a registry so each panel stays small.
- **Editor preferences.** Font size, tab width, word wrap, line numbers, and minimap, with a live preview — applied to every code editor in the app (scripts, request/response bodies).
- **Font choices, with a custom option.** Curated code-font and UI-font pickers with real previews, plus a VS Code-style **Custom** free-text field for any font installed on your system (with safe fallbacks). The interface preview reads like real Vayu output (`GET /users · 200 OK · 45ms`).
- **Configurable live dashboard.** A **time-based** retention window for the live charts (1m / 5m / 15m / 30m / Full run), a **capacity SLO threshold** that drives the breakpoint/saturation call-outs and the SLO line, adjustable **chart granularity** (0.5 / 1 / 2s), and a **live refresh rate**.
- **Data management & reset.** Clear stored run history, and reset all appearance/editor/dashboard preferences to their defaults — without touching your collections, requests, or run history.
- **Reduced motion.** An accessibility toggle that collapses transitions and animations across the app.

### Changed
- **One charting stack.** The hand-rolled SVG charts and the lone **recharts** history chart were removed in favor of the shared primitive; dropping `recharts` shrinks the charts bundle from **96.9 KB to 22.2 KB gzipped (~4.4× smaller)**, and Canvas rendering removes the point-count downsampling the old stacks needed on long runs.
- **Platform-aware shortcut hints.** Keyboard-shortcut indicators (the Dock, tooltips, and empty states) now render per-platform from one helper — Apple glyphs on macOS (`⇧⌘E`), words elsewhere (`Ctrl+Shift+E`).

### Fixed
- **Dark-mode contrast.** The toggle switch (off-state was near-invisible), keyboard key-caps, and destructive action buttons now read clearly in dark mode.
- **Settings survive an offline engine.** App Settings render even when the engine is down; only the engine-backed section shows the unavailable state instead of blanking the whole screen.
- **Code-font previews are actually distinct.** The offered faces (Fira Code, IBM Plex Mono, Space Mono) are now loaded, so the previews differ instead of all falling back to one font.
- **Chart cursor consistency.** Cursor sync is value-based so every chart lines up under one hover, and a duplicate x-axis label case was corrected.

[0.8.0]: https://github.com/athrvk/vayu/compare/v0.7.0...v0.8.0
